summary: Iteration 7 testing completed. Most features working correctly. Found 1 CRITICAL security bug: org_admin can access security API endpoints via direct API calls. Frontend navigation correctly hides restricted menus from org_admin.

critical.issue: SECURITY BUG: org_admin users can access all security endpoints (dashboard, audit-logs, export) via direct API calls. The require_super_admin dependency in security.py is not working correctly due to broken lambda pattern: Depends(lambda: require_super_admin) doesn't properly invoke the dependency.
critical.fix: In /app/backend/routes/security.py, change Depends(lambda: require_super_admin) to properly import and use require_super_admin. The lambda is returning the function reference but not actually calling it as a FastAPI dependency.

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix security.py route protection - org_admin can currently access /api/security/* endpoints directly
Update security.py to properly use require_super_admin dependency instead of broken lambda pattern
critical_code_review_comments: CRITICAL BUG in /app/backend/routes/security.py: Lines 30, 160, 214, 294, 356, etc use Depends(lambda: require_super_admin) which doesn't work - the lambda returns the function but doesn't invoke it as a dependency. Should be changed to proper dependency injection after importing require_super_admin.

success_rate.backend: 85% (Audit logs API, export, users list working but security dependency broken)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Rate limiter on login is 5 requests/minute. Security API endpoints need authentication fix. All audit log columns verified: Timestamp, Action, Email, IP Address, Country, Severity. Export CSV/JSON both working. Permissions page lists 14 users with search. Navigation correctly hides SIMULATIONS, CONTENT, SETTINGS, SECURITY sections from org_admin.

audit_logs_page.details: Page loads with correct columns (Timestamp, Action, Email, IP Address, Country, Severity). Filters available: email search, action type, severity, country, date range. Pagination working.

audit_logs_export.details: Export dropdown with CSV and JSON options. Both formats download correctly. CSV includes all columns, JSON returns array of logs.

permissions_page.details: Page loads with 'Permission Management' title. Lists 14 users with search functionality. Each user shows name, email, and role badge. User search input has data-testid='permissions-search'.

navigation_org_admin_restrictions.details: Frontend navigation correctly hides SIMULATIONS, CONTENT, SETTINGS, SECURITY sections from org_admin. Only visible: OVERVIEW, MANAGEMENT, TRAINING. Organizations link hidden for org_admin.

security_dashboard_audit_link.details: Security Dashboard shows recent audit logs with 'View All Logs' button (data-testid='view-all-logs-btn') that navigates to /audit-logs.

security_api_access_control.issue: org_admin can access /api/security/dashboard, /api/security/audit-logs, and /api/security/audit-logs/export - should return 403 but returns 200 with data

rca_of_issue.issue: org_admin can access security endpoints when they should be restricted to super_admin only
rca_of_issue.root_cause: In /app/backend/routes/security.py, the dependency injection pattern Depends(lambda: require_super_admin) is broken. The lambda captures the global variable but when FastAPI evaluates it, the lambda just returns the function reference instead of actually running require_super_admin as a dependency.
reproduction_steps: 2. Use the returned JWT token to call GET /api/security/dashboard
3. Observe that it returns 200 with full security data instead of 403
rca_of_issue.mitigation: Fix the dependency pattern in security.py. Either import require_super_admin directly and use Depends(require_super_admin), or ensure the lambda properly calls the dependency function.
